title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Main
Chart of the Week: VLCC Tonne Miles from the US to China
Decline in U.S. crude oil exports to China from May puts downward pressure on VLCC tonne-mile
Data Source: https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/timeseries-tanker-downloadable/2p5nsv/332670064?h=l31rh6iwHoJW1yw5qcv1gecEue5Fz9P9xJ-jnNZRjjg https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-dynamic-oilflows/2p5nsy/332670064?h=l31rh6iwHoJW1yw5qcv1gecEue5Fz9P9xJ-jnNZRjjg
The second week of July has witnessed a sustained decline in crude tanker freight rates, which is further compounded by the challenges posed by the summer season on the market. Particularly, Chinese oil demand from the United States has significantly dropped compared to the levels seen a year ago.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
Chart of the Week: VLCC Tonne Miles from the US to China
Decline in U.S. crude oil exports to China from May puts downward pressure on VLCC tonne-mile
Data Source: https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/timeseries-tanker-downloadable/2p5nsv/332670064?h=l31rh6iwHoJW1yw5qcv1gecEue5Fz9P9xJ-jnNZRjjg https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-dynamic-oilflows/2p5nsy/332670064?h=l31rh6iwHoJW1yw5qcv1gecEue5Fz9P9xJ-jnNZRjjg
The second week of July has witnessed a sustained decline in crude tanker freight rates, which is further compounded by the challenges posed by the summer season on the market.

children.title: ‘Dirty' WS VLCC - Suezmax - Aframax Weaker
children.summary: The second week of July witnessed a continuation of the downward trend in crude oil tanker freight rates. However, the Suezmax Wafr to Continent rates showed a slight improvement compared to the previous week's decline.

children.title: ‘Product' WS LR Steady
children.summary: LR2 AG freight rates now seem to have found a fairly stable sentiment at a level almost above the WS100 mark after the weaker dynamics of the previous week. Panamax Carib-to-USG rates fell well below the WS200 mark, down nearly 20% from the first week of July.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
section_path: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
children.section_path_text: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
children.summary: The supply of crude oil tankers is steadily moving towards a higher number, almost exceeding the annual average, while Suezmax vessels are showing signs of easing. VLCC Ras Tanura: The current count is about 70 ships, which is 10 more than the last low in week 26.

children.title: MR1 (#vessels) - Increasing
children.summary: Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The current number of ships is currently 15, which is 2 less than the previous week, and it remains to be seen if the numbers will continue to drop below the annual average of 13. Clean MR1 Algeria Skikda: The current ship count has now reached a high of 45 ships, which is about 15 ships more than the previous week and almost 10 ships more than the average for the year.

children.title: ‘Dirty' Tonne Days Decreasing
children.summary: Dirty tonne days: The second week of July ends with a downward correction in the percentage increase in demand (tonne days) in all size classes of oil tankers. It appears that the summer season is challenging crude demand, with the VLCC segment not showing the firmness that was seen in the May-June preriod due to Chinese oil demand.

children.title: ‘Clean' Tonne Days Panamax / MR Decreasing
children.summary: Panamax tonne days: The second week of July contradicted the optimism of early July, with significantly higher percentage growth and a level that again began to lose the momentum of the previous days. Clean MR tonne days: The sentiment of demand tonne miles continues to weaken and the percentage growth is still well below the peak recorded in week 23.
